items.label: MangoHud
items.description: MangoHud is a Vulkan and OpenGL overlay for monitoring FPS, frame time, CPU/GPU utilisation and temperatures. Prepends 'mangohud' to the launch options. Requires the 'mangohud' package to be installed.

conflictsWith.reason: MangoApp is gamescope's built-in overlay — running both MangoApp and the MangoHud wrapper at the same time produces duplicate overlays.

items.label: GameMode
items.description: Feral Interactive's GameMode requests CPU governor changes, scheduler hints, and GPU performance profiles for the duration of the game. Prepends 'gamemode' to the launch options. Requires the 'gamemode' package (gamemoded must be running).

items.label: game-performance
items.description: KDE Plasma / distro-specific performance profile wrapper. Sets a higher CPU performance governor and other power management tweaks for the duration of the game. Requires the 'power-profiles-daemon' or equivalent.

items.label: Proton log file
items.description: Writes a detailed Wine/Proton log to ~/steam-<appid>.log. Essential for debugging startup failures or missing DLLs. Disabling after investigation avoids large disk writes.

items.label: DXVK HUD (fps)
items.description: Enables the DXVK on-screen HUD showing current FPS. Requires DXVK to be active (default in Proton). For a full HUD set DXVK_HUD=full instead of fps.

conflictsWith.reason: DXVK HUD has no effect when WineD3D replaces DXVK.

items.label: WineD3D (OpenGL)
items.description: Forces Wine's WineD3D OpenGL-based Direct3D implementation instead of DXVK. Useful when Vulkan drivers are broken or unavailable. Performance is generally lower than DXVK.

conflictsWith.reason: DXVK HUD has no effect when WineD3D replaces DXVK.
DXVK low-latency path is irrelevant when WineD3D is used instead of DXVK.
PROTON_NO_D3D11 is a DXVK bypass — WineD3D already replaces DXVK for D3D11.

items.label: Disable esync
items.description: Disables Proton's eventfd-based synchronisation (esync). Try this if you experience stutter, hangs, or sync-related crashes. Some games perform better without esync.

items.label: Disable fsync
items.description: Disables Proton's futex-based synchronisation (fsync). Try this if you experience stutter or sync-related issues not resolved by disabling esync. Requires a kernel with futex2 support.

items.label: Disable D3D11
items.description: Prevents Proton from initialising DXVK's D3D11 layer. Niche troubleshooting — some very old games that mistakenly try D3D11 but need D3D9 will fail otherwise. Avoid unless you have a specific reason.

conflictsWith.reason: WineD3D already replaces DXVK for D3D11 — PROTON_NO_D3D11 is redundant.

items.label: DXVK low latency
items.description: Enables a low-latency frame pacing path inside DXVK. Availability and effect depend on the DXVK version bundled with the Proton build. May reduce input latency at the cost of slightly lower average FPS.

conflictsWith.reason: DXVK low-latency path is irrelevant when WineD3D is used instead of DXVK.

items.label: vkBasalt
items.description: Enables the vkBasalt post-processing Vulkan layer. Allows reshade-style effects (sharpening, SMAA, CAS) on top of any Vulkan game. Requires vkBasalt to be installed and ~/.config/vkBasalt/vkBasalt.conf to be configured.

items.label: Disable Steam Deck profile
items.description: Sets SteamDeck=0 so the game does not detect a Steam Deck. Some titles switch to a simplified Deck-only UI or skip certain quality settings when on Deck. Use this to restore the standard desktop experience.

items.label: Proton Wayland
items.description: Enables Proton's experimental native Wayland output path (bypasses XWayland). Can fix tearing or scaling issues on Wayland compositors. May break overlays, clipboard, or mouse input in some games.

items.label: Disable Steam Input
items.description: Disables Proton's Steam Input controller remapping layer. Try this if your controller is double-mapped, or if the Steam overlay interferes with in-game controller detection.

items.label: Prefer SDL controller
items.description: Tells Proton to prefer SDL-based controller handling over DirectInput. Useful as a workaround for gamepads not detected correctly in some DirectInput games.

items.label: Local shader cache
items.description: Keeps the DXVK/VKD3D shader cache per-game rather than in the shared Steam shader cache. Useful to avoid cache poisoning between games and to debug shader compilation problems.

items.label: HDR output
items.description: Instructs Proton / Wine to attempt HDR output (PROTON_ENABLE_HDR=1). Requires an HDR-capable display and a game that supports HDR. Often combined with Gamescope HDR.

items.label: Prefer FSync over NTSync
items.description: Sets PROTON_USE_NTSYNC=0 to disable NTSync and fall back to FSync. Some ProtonPlus/ProtonGE builds default to NTSync; this forces the older FSync path which may be more stable on certain kernels.

items.label: Scopebuddy Auto HDR
items.description: Enables Scopebuddy's automatic HDR tonemapping (SCB_AUTO_HDR=1). Only has effect when the scopebuddy wrapper is active. Requires Scopebuddy to be installed.

items.label: Scopebuddy Auto VRR
items.description: Enables Scopebuddy's automatic Variable Refresh Rate control (SCB_AUTO_VRR=1). Only has effect when the scopebuddy wrapper is active.

items.label: Mesa Anti-Lag
items.description: Enables AMD's VK_AMD_anti_lag Vulkan extension via the implicit VkLayer_MESA_anti_lag layer. Paces CPU submission to prevent it from getting too far ahead of the GPU, reducing input-to-screen latency. Requires Mesa 25.3+ with the anti-lag layer built in. Can cause crashes on Intel Arc and NVIDIA.

conflictsWith.reason: Cannot enable and disable the Mesa anti-lag layer simultaneously — only one of these flags should be set at a time.

items.label: Disable Mesa Anti-Lag
items.description: Sets DISABLE_LAYER_MESA_ANTI_LAG=1 to explicitly suppress the implicit anti-lag Vulkan layer. Use this if games crash after Mesa 25.3 was installed — commonly reported on Intel Arc and NVIDIA setups with mixed Mesa drivers.

conflictsWith.reason: Cannot enable and disable the Mesa anti-lag layer simultaneously — only one of these flags should be set at a time.

items.label: Use AMD dGPU (DRI_PRIME)
items.description: Sets DRI_PRIME=1 on hybrid graphics systems (iGPU + dGPU). Forces the game to run on the discrete AMD GPU instead of the integrated one. Required on laptops with AMD dGPUs that use PRIME offloading.

items.label: Hide AMD APU
items.description: Sets PROTON_HIDE_APU=1 to mask the AMD APU from Proton's GPU detection. Some games misidentify integrated AMD graphics as a dedicated GPU and apply wrong presets. Use this if the game defaults to low quality despite having a dGPU.

conflictsWith.reason: You can only hide one GPU vendor at a time — hiding both AMD APU and NVIDIA GPU simultaneously is contradictory.

items.label: FSR 4 upgrade
items.description: Enables PROTON_FSR4_UPGRADE=1 — upgrades FSR 1/2/3 upscaling calls to FSR 4 in supported Proton builds. Bleeding-edge feature; requires a Proton version that supports FSR 4 (Proton 9.0+ or ProtonGE). May cause visual artefacts.

items.label: FSR 4 RDNA3 upgrade
items.description: Enables PROTON_FSR4_RDNA3_UPGRADE=1 — activates the RDNA3-optimised FSR 4 path. Requires an RDNA3 GPU (RX 7000 series) and must be used together with the base FSR 4 upgrade flag.

implies.reason: The RDNA3-specific FSR 4 path requires the base FSR 4 upgrade to also be active.

items.label: NVAPI (NVIDIA)
items.description: Enables PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 — exposes NVIDIA's NVAPI to the Windows game, enabling DLSS, Reflex, and other NVIDIA-specific paths. Required for DLSS to work in most games. Only effective with an NVIDIA GPU.

conflictsWith.reason: Hiding the NVIDIA GPU while exposing NVAPI to the game is contradictory — NVAPI requires the GPU to be visible.

items.label: Update DLSS (NGX)
items.description: Enables PROTON_ENABLE_NGX_UPDATER=1 — allows Proton to update the NVIDIA NGX / DLSS libraries to the latest version available from NVIDIA. Useful to get the newest DLSS 3/4 frame generation in older games.

implies.reason: NGX updater requires NVAPI to be enabled to expose the NVIDIA library interface.

conflictsWith.reason: NGX updater requires the NVIDIA GPU to be visible.

items.label: Hide NVIDIA GPU
items.description: Sets PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU=1 — masks the NVIDIA GPU from the game's GPU detection. Workaround for titles that refuse to run on NVIDIA or that behave incorrectly when an NVIDIA GPU is detected.

conflictsWith.reason: You can only hide one GPU vendor at a time — hiding both NVIDIA GPU and AMD APU simultaneously is contradictory.
Hiding the NVIDIA GPU while exposing NVAPI to the game is contradictory — NVAPI requires the GPU to be visible.
NGX updater requires the NVIDIA GPU to be visible.

items.label: DLSS indicator
items.description: Enables PROTON_DLSS_INDICATOR=1 — shows an on-screen indicator when DLSS is active. Useful to confirm DLSS is working correctly after enabling NVAPI.

items.label: NVIDIA libraries
items.description: Enables PROTON_NVIDIA_LIBS=1 — exposes additional NVIDIA libraries (PhysX, CUDA bindings) to the game. May help with games that require PhysX hardware acceleration or CUDA compute.

items.label: XeSS upgrade
items.description: Enables PROTON_XESS_UPGRADE=1 — upgrades Intel XeSS calls to a newer version of the XeSS SDK inside Proton. Works on all GPU vendors. May improve image quality in games that use older XeSS versions.

items.label: Fullscreen (-f)
items.description: Passes the -f / --fullscreen flag to gamescope, requesting a fullscreen nested compositor window. Useful to force fullscreen presentation regardless of the game's own window mode.

items.label: Borderless window (-b)
items.description: Passes -b / --borderless to gamescope, displaying the compositor in a borderless (decoration-free) window. Useful when you want windowed mode but without the title bar.

items.label: Steam integration (-e)
items.description: Passes -e / --steam to gamescope, enabling Steam integration mode. Gamescope will close automatically when the game exits and reports game state back to Steam.

items.label: Force Windows fullscreen
items.description: Passes --force-windows-fullscreen to gamescope. Forces Windows games that open in a borderless windowed mode to appear truly fullscreen inside gamescope.

items.label: VRR / Adaptive Sync (-O)
items.description: Passes -O / --adaptive-sync to gamescope, enabling Variable Refresh Rate on the display. Requires a VRR-capable monitor and a compositor that supports VRR. Reduces perceived stutter at lower frame rates.

items.label: HDR (--hdr-enabled)
items.description: Passes --hdr-enabled to gamescope, requesting HDR output from the compositor. Requires an HDR-capable display and a Wayland compositor that supports the HDR protocol. Also enabling PROTON_ENABLE_HDR=1 is highly recommended so the game actually outputs HDR signal.

implies.reason: Gamescope HDR compositing is only useful when the game also outputs an HDR signal — PROTON_ENABLE_HDR=1 enables this.

items.label: HDR inverse tone-mapping (--hdr-itm-enable)
items.description: Passes --hdr-itm-enable to gamescope, enabling inverse tone-mapping that converts SDR game output to HDR. Useful for games that don't natively support HDR. Works best with hdr-itm-target-nits set.

items.label: HDR ITM target nits
items.description: Sets the target peak brightness in nits for inverse tone-mapping (--hdr-itm-target-nits <N>). The default is typically 1000 nits. Increase for brighter HDR displays.

items.label: SDR content brightness (nits)
items.description: Sets the brightness level in nits that gamescope uses when displaying SDR content on an HDR display (--hdr-sdr-content-nits <N>). Default is around 400 nits.

items.label: SDR gamut wideness
items.description: Sets the colour gamut wideness for SDR content on HDR displays (--sdr-gamut-wideness <0-1>). 0 = sRGB, 1 = native display gamut. Values between 0 and 1 blend between the two.

items.label: Output width (-W)
items.description: Sets the gamescope output (compositor) width in pixels with -W <N>. This is the resolution the compositor presents to the display. Use together with Output height. Leave empty to match the display's native width.

items.label: Output height (-H)
items.description: Sets the gamescope output (compositor) height in pixels with -H <N>. Use together with Output width to define a custom output resolution. Leave empty to match the display's native height.

items.label: Game (nested) width (-w)
items.description: Sets the resolution that gamescope renders the game at with -w <N>. This is the game's rendering resolution, which gamescope then upscales to the output resolution. Leave empty to use the output width.

items.label: Game (nested) height (-h)
items.description: Sets the height that gamescope renders the game at with -h <N>. Use together with Game width for a custom rendering resolution that gamescope then upscales.

items.label: Upscale mode (-S)
items.description: Sets the upscaling algorithm gamescope uses to scale the game resolution to the output resolution (-S <mode>). 'auto' lets gamescope decide, 'integer' uses pixel-perfect integer scaling, 'fit' fits without cropping, 'fill' fills with cropping, 'stretch' ignores aspect ratio.

items.label: Upscale filter (-F)
items.description: Sets the upscaling filter gamescope uses (-F <filter>). 'linear' is bilinear, 'nearest' is nearest-neighbour (sharp pixels), 'fsr' uses AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution, 'nis' uses NVIDIA Image Scaling, 'pixel' is a pixel art filter.

items.label: FSR sharpness (--sharpness)
items.description: Sets the FSR sharpness level (--sharpness <0-20>). Only effective when the upscale filter is set to 'fsr'. Higher values = sharper image. Default is typically 2.

items.label: NIS sharpness (--nis-sharpness)
items.description: Sets the NIS (NVIDIA Image Scaling) sharpness level (--nis-sharpness <0-1>). Only effective when the upscale filter is 'nis'. 0 = no sharpening, 1 = maximum sharpening.

items.label: Frame cap (-r)
items.description: Sets a frame rate cap inside gamescope with -r <N> FPS. Gamescope paces frames at the compositor level rather than relying on the game's own limiter, producing smoother results. Set to 0 or leave empty for unlimited.

items.label: Immediate flips (--immediate-flips)
items.description: Passes --immediate-flips to gamescope, presenting frames immediately without waiting for vblank. Can reduce input latency at the cost of potential tearing. Useful for competitive gaming.

items.label: Realtime scheduling (--rt)
items.description: Passes --rt / --realtime to gamescope, requesting real-time CPU scheduling priority for the compositor thread. Can reduce stutter but requires appropriate system permissions (e.g. gamescope in the realtime group).

items.label: MangoApp overlay (--mangoapp)
items.description: Passes --mangoapp to gamescope, enabling the built-in mangoapp performance overlay. This is gamescope's native overlay and is more integrated than the external MangoHud wrapper. Do not use together with the MangoHud wrapper.

conflictsWith.reason: MangoApp is gamescope's built-in overlay — running both MangoApp and the MangoHud wrapper at the same time produces duplicate overlays.

items.label: Expose Wayland (--expose-wayland)
items.description: Passes --expose-wayland to gamescope, exposing a Wayland socket inside the gamescope session. Allows Wayland-native applications to run inside gamescope.

items.label: Prefer output (--prefer-output)
items.description: Pins gamescope to a specific display output connector (--prefer-output <name>, e.g. 'DP-1' or 'HDMI-A-1'). Useful on multi-monitor setups to ensure the game runs on the correct display.

items.label: Force grab cursor (--force-grab-cursor)
items.description: Passes --force-grab-cursor to gamescope, locking the mouse cursor inside the gamescope window. Useful for games that improperly release the cursor and cause it to escape the game window.

items.label: Mouse sensitivity (--mouse-sensitivity)
items.description: Scales mouse input by the given factor inside gamescope (--mouse-sensitivity <factor>). Values above 1.0 increase sensitivity, values below 1.0 decrease it. Default is 1.0 (no change).
